text.text:
Pretty disappointed. We made a special trip all the way from the Bay Area just to try this place.

The purple rice ball and the regular rice ball were both generously sized, and the fried egg inside was pretty good. The sausage, however, had a strange flavor that I didn’t really enjoy. Not so bad.. just a little… not fresh?

The service felt awkward. The staff seemed unfriendly before I even had a chance to ask any questions. We also asked whether the rice dumplings were Northern- or Southern-style, but the person at the counter couldn’t tell.

We ordered several other dishes as well. Aside from the bowl cake, which was fairly ordinary, the rest of the food was actually decent if you’re judging only the taste and setting aside the price and the service.

text.text:
So let’s see if they’re pretty good eh or ew. I also got their beef roll which was rolled in a chewy scallion pancake and was PACKED with meaty, savory, a bit woody shredded beef with fragrant green onions and cilantro. It was a bit on the salty end and Would’ve been better if it also had hoisin sauce and pickled veggies for some extra sweetness and crunch tho. I got the viral fan Tuan with pretty purple chewy sticky rice, sweet savory pork floss, crunchy pickled veggies, soft radish, flavorful green onion eggs, fatty sweet Taiwanese sausage, and Chinese donut that kinda got soggy but still had those yummy oily flaves. Overall this place was pretty good.

Beef roll $14
Fan Tuan $11.75

text.text:
Awesome Taiwanese style breakfast. If u have never tried chinese donut, doesnt get much better than this place. If u get sugar on your donut it can be a delicious dessert.

Purple rice roll is amazing. Would be a great thing to pack for a hike. Would keep nicely and packs very well.

Salty soy milk with donuts is very comforting. I really liked it with some chili oil.

Great spot!

originalText.text:

Salty soy milk with donuts is very comforting. I really liked it with some chili oil.

Great spot!

text.text:
Popular Taiwanese restaurant with brunch menu. My date prefers food that reminds him of his hometown and recommended it for breakfast. I went with the fuzzy notion there would be soy milk and something donut-adjacent. It was pretty much what I expected, plus a couple nice surprises.

We ordered exclusively from the brunch menu. Rice rolls were yummy. So were the donuts. I expected them to be sweet. My date expected them to be salty. They were a little salty, not really. Was surprised by how much sugar my date dumped in the soy milk, but he was right, it was good that way. Kinda bland without, not bad though. The beef rolls were good. A little dry, much yummier with chili sauce.

Pleasantly surprised by how affordable everything was. Much better deal than typical egg sandwich places I frequent. Also, so much meat! Normally, I’m charged $2 just to glimpse the sausage. Not here. Protein for days. Even milk was high protein, my date reminded me.

We sat down, looked at menus, my date ordered at the counter. We ate and left, no waiting for the bill—it was prepaid.

Breakfast food was so different from my usual fare, it felt like lunch. Was yummy and affordable. Would go back, if my partner wanted to. Solid brunch menu.

FYI women’s restroom is back left facing the restaurant. Men’s is back right, down the hall. Sign says customers only.

text.text: This spot is a must try! Really enjoyed their purple rice roll. Its all the hype and rightfully so. Get the fried donut extra crispy, add green onion pancake and brown sugar with their sasuage. That made it so so good. I also really enjoyed their beef roll with green onion pancake as it was packed full of flavor!
